title: Abdollah Khan appointed governor of Khuzestan; titles granted to sons
description: The shah appointed Abdollah Khan governor of Khuzestan and titled him Sardar Akram. At his request Hossein Qoli Khan received Sa'ed al-Saltaneh and Mohtaj Ali Khan received Ejlal al-Mamalek.

title: Gharagozloo participation in Mohammad Shah’s Herat campaign
description: Thousands of Gharagozloo troops served under Nabi Khan. Abdollah Khan supported his force; Gharagozloo troops helped assault the fortifications. Nabi Khan was killed after severe wounds.

title: British occupation of Bushehr and dispersal of the Gharagozloo guard
description: British forces attacked Bushehr to pressure Iran to withdraw from Herat. The fifty-man Gharagozloo guard was driven off because of its small size.

title: Gharagozloo participation in the Anglo-Persian War in southern Iran
description: Mostafa Qoli Khan, Rustam Khan, and Ali Khan served against British forces around Bandar Lengeh, Bushehr, Dalaki, and Borazjan.

title: Defeat of Nasrallah Khan of Laristan
description: Ali Naqi Khan led the Dargazin regiment and defeated Nasrallah Khan in Bikhuh district.

title: Gharagozloo military service in Kerman
description: Ali Khan Nosrat al-Molk and other Gharagozloo officers served in Kerman; Mohammad Hossein Khan later received the title Hosam al-Molk.

title: Marriage of Gholamreza Khan and Qamar al-Saltaneh
description: A lavish dynastic marriage arranged by Zeyn al-Abedin Khan Hosam al-Molk.

title: Arrest of Hamadan Gharagozloo amir-tumans
description: Following unrest in Hamadan, Hosam al-Molk, Saed al-Saltaneh, and Zia al-Molk were dismissed and detained.

title: Hosam al-Molk appointed governor of Kermanshah
description: After extensive attempts to obtain provincial office, Zeyn al-Abedin Khan Hosam al-Molk was appointed governor of Kermanshah.

title: Aqa Jan Gharagozloo and the Russian embassy case
description: After Pavlov was killed in an altercation involving Aqa Jan’s servant, Aqa Jan fled, was arrested in Hamadan, imprisoned in Tehran, and handed to the Russian embassy.

title: Gharagozloo regiments sent against unrest in Shiraz
description: Gharagozloo regiments under Saed al-Saltaneh were sent to suppress unrest in Shiraz; seven or eight people were killed.

title: Mahmoud Khan appointed colonel and successor to Nabi Khan’s command
description: Mohammad Shah appointed Mahmoud Khan colonel and placed Nabi Khan’s former troops under his command.

title: Mahmoud Khan mission to Russia
description: Mahmoud Khan served as Iranian envoy to Russia and returned with promotion and royal honors.

title: Mahmoud Khan steam spinning factory project
description: Mahmoud Khan imported machinery from Moscow for a modern steam-powered spinning factory; the project failed for lack of trained operators.

title: Mahmoud Khan appointed first minister of commerce
description: Mahmoud Khan Naser al-Molk became the first holder of the newly established ministry of commerce.

title: Deaths of major Gharagozloo khans
description: The book records the deaths of Abdollah Khan Sarem al-Saltaneh, Ali Naqi Khan, Ali Khan Nosrat al-Molk, Amanollah Khan, Mostafa Qoli Khan, Mahmoud Khan, and Mohammad Hossein Khan Hosam al-Molk.

title: Abdollah Khan appointed governor of Khuzestan
description: Abdollah Khan Saed al-Saltaneh was appointed governor and received the title Sardar Akram.

title: Foundation of the Islamiyyeh School at Shervin
description: Hosam al-Molk and Zia al-Molk established the Islamiyyeh School.

title: Gharagozloo grain blockade and Hamadan bread crisis
description: Gharagozloo landowners restricted grain deliveries during conflict with the Majles-e Favaed-e Ammeh and Constitutionalists.

title: Killing of Seyyed Jamal al-Din Vaez Esfahani
description: The preacher was delivered to Amir Afkham’s circle and killed in Borujerd.

title: Gharagozloo regiments against Tabriz Constitutionalists
description: Regiments commanded by Sardar Akram, Mansur al-Dowleh, and Amir Afkham supported Mohammad Ali Shah against Tabriz.

title: Gharagozloo participation in the Salar al-Dowleh crisis
description: Amir Nezam and other khans shifted positions during Salar al-Dowleh’s rebellion; Amir Afkham later joined government forces.

title: Naser al-Molk dissolves the Second Majles
description: As regent, Naser al-Molk ordered Yeprem Khan to close the Second Majles during the Russian ultimatum crisis.

title: Russian occupation of Hamadan and appointment of Amir Afkham
description: Russian forces occupied Hamadan with assistance from Amir Afkham, who was installed as governor.

title: Arrest and release of Amir Nezam after the 1921 coup
description: Amir Nezam was arrested by Seyyed Zia’s government and reportedly paid 25,000 tomans to secure release.

title: Gharagozloo participation in the 1304 SH Constituent Assembly
description: Zia al-Molk Farmand represented Hamadan in the assembly that confirmed Reza Shah.

title: Birth of Naser al-Molk
description: Born to Ahmad Khan Sartip; grandson of Mirza Mahmud Khan

title: Oxford education
description: Studied political science and mastered English and French

title: First audience with Naser al-Din Shah
description: Introduced through Amin al-Dowleh and grandfather

title: Granted title Moshir-e Hozur
description: Granted at grandfather's request

title: Appointed head of Khorasan forces
description: Military-administrative appointment

title: Appointed royal aide-de-camp
description: Aide-de-camp to Naser al-Din Shah

title: Inherited Naser al-Molk title and Hamadan governorship
description: Succeeded grandfather; received royal decoration and portrait

title: Third royal European journey
description: Accompanied Naser al-Din Shah and translated foreign material

title: Ottoman and Paris financial mission
description: Mission related to financial consequences of the Tobacco Concession

title: Extraordinary embassy announcing Mozaffar al-Din Shah
description: Sultan Abdolhamid II gave ceremonial sword for the shah

title: Appointed head of royal arsenal
description: Delegated daily management to Hossein-Qoli Khan Zia al-Molk

title: Appointed Minister of Finance
description: Served in Amin al-Dowleh cabinet

title: Removed after Amin al-Dowleh's fall
description: Lost finance portfolio

title: Governor of Kurdistan
description: Governorship enabled closer supervision of Hamadan estates

title: Letter opposing immediate constitutionalism
description: Argued Iran lacked educated personnel for parliament and full equality

title: Hamadan grain/bread crisis
description: Large landlords accused of withholding grain; Naser al-Molk among named khans

title: Nominated and appointed prime minister
description: Chosen by First Majles after fall of Mirza Ahmad Khan Moshir al-Saltaneh

title: Presented 'family cabinet'
description: Cabinet members had extensive kinship links

title: Royal oath to Constitution
description: Naser al-Molk arranged attendance and oath of shah and princes

title: First parliamentary national budget reforms
description: Reduced royal, princely, and court budgets

title: Resignation submitted during crisis
description: Shah initially refused resignation

title: Arrest and threatened execution
description: Mohammad Ali Shah ordered detention; British intervention saved him

title: Departure to Europe
description: Exile/departure required by shah

title: Refused later request to form cabinet
description: Mirza Javad Khan Sa'd al-Dowleh took charge instead

title: Foreign minister in two Sepahdar cabinets
description: Served in first and second cabinets of Mohammad Vali Khan Sepahdar Tonekaboni

title: Election as regent
description: Won 40 of 71 votes; rival was Mostowfi al-Mamalek

title: Return to Tehran as regent
description: Returned via Russian territory

title: Suppression of Mohammad Ali Shah restoration attempt
description: Regency faced rebellion of ex-shah, Salar al-Dowleh, and Shoa al-Saltaneh

title: Russian ultimatum and Shuster crisis
description: Russia demanded Shuster's dismissal

title: Closure of Second Majles
description: Yeprem Khan closed parliament on Naser al-Molk's order

title: Acceptance of ultimatum and dismissal of Shuster
description: Followed closure of parliament

title: Long stay abroad while regent
description: Continued receiving regency salary and estate income

title: Enthronement of Ahmad Shah
description: Used birth date recorded on Quran to end minority and regency

title: Return after fall of Qajar dynasty
description: Returned to Iran after Qajar deposition

title: Death of Naser al-Molk
description: Died in Tehran

title: Divorce of Hossein Ali and Iran Khanom
description: Hossein Ali paid 10,000 tomans to divorce Teymurtash's daughter

title: Conspiracy against Qizil Arslan
description: Sultan Tughril III asked Amir Gharagoz to kill Qizil Arslan during a reception, then abandoned the plan; after disclosure, Gharagoz was blinded.

title: Fighting in Hamadan after Qizil Arslan's death
description: Badr al-Din Gharagoz and the Atabeg faction fought supporters of Majd al-Dowleh in Hamadan; the narrative says a severe earthquake interrupted the fighting.

title: Amir Jafar Ali serves Mohammad-Zaman Mirza
description: Amir Jafar Ali Gharagoz appears among the commanders of Mohammad-Zaman Mirza in the struggles around Balkh and Gharjistan.

title: Killing of Amir Jafar Ali Gharagoz
description: Sent as an envoy during negotiations, Amir Jafar Ali was killed by Ebrahim Sultan Musellu.

title: Amir Mohammad Gharagoz accompanies Humayun
description: Amir Mohammad Gharagoz is named among officers moving with Humayun from Qandahar toward Kabul.

title: Ebrahim Khan appointed governor of Kerman
description: After earlier governorships in Astarabad, Gilan, and Mazandaran, Ebrahim Khan Gharagozloo was appointed to Kerman.

title: Resistance to Afghan forces near Tabriz
description: A report names Kalb Ali Qasem Aqa Gharagozloo as a leader of defenders who took refuge in a small fortress and were later killed after a false promise of safety.

title: Defeat of Sarfaraz Beg Khodabandehlu after Nader Shah's death
description: Gharagozloo forces defended their Hamadan holdings, defeated Sarfaraz Beg, and destroyed his force after months of conflict.

title: Gharagozloo alliance with Karim Khan Zand
description: Shahbaz Khan, Mohammad-Hossein Khan, and Nasrollah Khan are associated with the Gharagozloo decision to support Karim Khan Zand.

title: Mohammad-Hossein Khan granted government of Zahab
description: The Zand administration granted Mohammad-Hossein Khan authority over Zahab.

title: Gharagozloo forces sent toward Yazd
description: Nasrollah Khan and Ebrahim Khan Gharagozloo commanded forces sent by Ali-Morad Khan to assist the governor of Yazd.

title: Mohammad-Hossein Khan sent to Kohgiluyeh
description: A document records forces under Mohammad-Hossein Khan Gharagozloo being sent to Kohgiluyeh.
